summary: REVISE. Authentication passed for the contract, dependency handoff, nested ledger, and all 34 declared context items, but the launchable prelaunch handoff validator accepts escaped lone UTF-16 surrogates contrary to R20. Candidate A must not be approved from this review.

artifacts.name: security-trace-review.md
artifacts.content:
# Security Trace Review

## Outcome

REVISE. Candidate A must not be approved, integrated, or merged from this review. The security-trace criterion fails because the external-host prelaunch handoff validator accepts malformed Unicode that requirement R20 requires it to reject before a handoff becomes launchable evidence.

## Authenticated Review Basis

- The specialist contract was authenticated before use at 23,012 raw bytes and sha256:d1a24a33cf03ccf624ee7fb76cc0f83b91cf5063c505a40f467cc2dbd1edb2b3.
- The prepare.candidate dependency handoff was authenticated before use at 32,202 raw bytes and sha256:f12b044d9398bc270d7e8d79e52d077296df3aecd7dd251d532a77aadd10b045.
- Its sole inline review-candidate-digests.json artifact was independently re-encoded as UTF-8 and matched 24,357 bytes and sha256:47920dc4ea5002a7c82e21d04fcab94e64a1501f25ca25d9d02af17c5ed024f2.
- All 34 context items declared by this blueprint independently matched their raw byte counts, SHA-256 digests, repository locator paths, read scopes, authority read scopes, filesystem.read permission scopes, and corresponding dependency-ledger records. The dependency ledger contains 35 sources; its only extra source is context.readme, which this specialist did not consume.
- Candidate identities are team.e016556b8933ef6ead3b1d321bf187cee6a286ca7ca19332dcf6505fe1d02a7d, compilation sha256:a2c43d26932e169707129065da52bdbef0b6e525a95cd0adf30d7a22c71ae807, and package sha256:d273142d00b9b960c99afb61d0e4236dec94b48d074a579dbef1ce8d486a94ed. Their domain-separated verification is an external-host launch fact, not a semantic PASS inferred by this specialist.
- The dependency ledger explicitly records semanticReview.performed false. This review therefore does not inherit a security conclusion from Wave 1 or Audit B.

## Finding

### ST-01 High: Escaped Lone Surrogates Pass The Launchable Handoff Validator

Requirement R20 in docs/specs/v11-specialist-compiler/evidence/dogfood/inputs/spec-before-integration.md:56 requires malformed Unicode to be rejected before handoffs become launchable evidence. The compiler satisfies that boundary for compiler input through hasLoneSurrogate in src/specialist-compiler.ts:168-200, but the external-host handoff path does not apply the same scalar-value check.

The strict control parser reaches JSON.parse at src/json.ts:140. Standard JSON.parse accepts an escaped lone surrogate such as \ud800 as a JavaScript string containing code unit D800. scripts/run-v11-dogfood.mjs:628-655 then validates handoff strings only for selected C0/C1, DEL, and bidi controls plus a UTF-8 byte ceiling. D800 is not in that rejection set. Buffer.byteLength converts the lone surrogate to a three-byte replacement encoding for measurement, so safeHandoffText returns true.

A read-only in-memory probe reproduced the exact predicate result:

- parsed code unit: d800
- measured UTF-8 bytes: 3
- containsUnsafeHandoffCharacters equivalent: false
- safeHandoffText equivalent: true

A structurally closed, raw-digest-bound PrelaunchAuditHandoff can therefore carry an escaped lone high or low surrogate in summary, assumptions, risks, followUps, or the required Markdown artifact while passing the host validator. Later UTF-8 materialization replaces that non-scalar value, so consumers can observe bytes or text different from the parsed in-memory value. More importantly, the handoff is launchable evidence despite an explicit fail-closed acceptance requirement.

Required correction:

1. Add a Unicode scalar-value check to safeHandoffText, preferably the same explicit high/low surrogate scan used by the compiler.
2. Add host regression cases using JSON escapes for unpaired high and low surrogates in every free-text handoff surface, including artifact content.
3. Verify that malformed handoffs fail before semantic PASS is accepted or a launch authorization is returned.
4. Replay the complete revision gate and issue new immutable candidate identities if any bound source changes.

## Controls Reviewed Before Stop

- Schema closure: specialist input objects are closed with additionalProperties false at concrete boundaries; repository and external context variants close readScope correctly; package-owned schema references and provider/runtime exclusion tests are present.
- Authority containment: Module and capability demand is checked against owner ceilings; filesystem permission scopes must exactly equal work-unit read/write scopes; context use is allowlisted and read-scope covered; blueprint authority is the deterministic union of validated owned work units. No owner-authority widening was found before the stop.
- Privacy and diagnostics: compiler strings and property keys are scanned for high-confidence secrets, controls, bidi characters, byte bounds, and lone surrogates before schema diagnostics; diagnostic pointers suppress unsafe or secret-bearing tokens. ST-01 shows this protection is not preserved at the external handoff boundary.
- Canonical digests: canonical JSON sorts object keys, rejects non-finite numbers and lone surrogates, and uses length-framed domain separation. Compilation rendering recompiles normalized input and requires canonical equality.
- Package tamper resistance: verification parses exactly one compilation.json, rerenders the complete package, requires canonical equality, and compares externally supplied compilation and package expectations. Raw file SHA-256 and byte metadata are bound into the manifest and package root. No reviewed-digest retention bypass was found before the stop.
- Rendering containment: dynamic Markdown fences exceed the longest backtick run in contained JSON; schema-safe identifiers occupy headings and paths; user text remains in dynamically fenced JSON; aggregate rendered contents are bounded. No Markdown breakout was found before the stop.
- External-host boundary: the docs correctly assign context delivery, permission enforcement, process execution, approval, and persistence to the host, and the launch authorization binds both package pairs plus exact receipt and handoff bytes. ST-01 prevents this boundary from satisfying fail-closed malformed-Unicode handling.

The contract requires stopping on a finding. This report makes no PASS claim for Candidate A or for review paths not completed after ST-01.

evidence.statement: The criterion does not pass: scripts/run-v11-dogfood.mjs safeHandoffText accepts escaped lone surrogates, violating the malformed-Unicode fail-closed requirement for launchable handoffs.

assumptions: The external-host launch facts are trusted only for the already verified domain-separated compilation and package identity pair; this specialist independently authenticated every raw artifact it was instructed and authorized to consume.
The R20 term malformed Unicode includes unpaired UTF-16 surrogates, consistent with the compiler implementation at src/specialist-compiler.ts:168-200 and the canonical JSON scalar-value rule.
risks: A raw-digest-bound PrelaunchAuditHandoff can pass host validation while containing a non-scalar string that changes to a replacement character on UTF-8 materialization.
Because the contract requires stopping on the first finding, no PASS is asserted for review paths not completed after ST-01.

failedAttempts.command: node -e in-memory lone-surrogate probe, quoting variant 1
failedAttempts.reason: Windows command-line quoting removed the JSON string wrapper and JSON.parse raised SyntaxError.

failedAttempts.command: node -e in-memory lone-surrogate probe, quoting variant 2
failedAttempts.reason: A second raw-template quoting form still lost the JSON string wrapper and JSON.parse raised SyntaxError.

failedAttempts.command: node -e in-memory lone-surrogate probe, quoting variant 3
failedAttempts.reason: The parser portion succeeded, but command-line quote removal turned the utf8 encoding label into an identifier and raised ReferenceError.

followUps: Add an explicit lone-high/lone-low-surrogate rejection to scripts/run-v11-dogfood.mjs safeHandoffText before any PrelaunchAuditHandoff can authorize launch.
Add regression cases with escaped unpaired high and low surrogates in summary, assumptions, risks, followUps, and artifact content, and require launch authorization verification to reject each case.
Replay the complete revision-19 gate and regenerate every affected digest-bound package, approval, receipt, authorization, and handoff after the fix.

commands.command: PowerShell Get-Item/Get-FileHash for the specialist contract
PowerShell UTF-8 byte and SHA-256 calculation for inline review-candidate-digests.json
PowerShell contract-driven authentication of 34 context files plus authority and dependency-ledger correspondence
rg/Get-Content static review over the exact declared schema, compiler, privacy, digest, renderer, host, contract, and test sources
node -e successful read-only lone-surrogate predicate probe

checks.statement: Concrete specialist input object boundaries are closed and package-owned schema references are used.
No blueprint path reviewed before the stop widened owner-approved Module, capability, context, filesystem scope, or permission demand.
Compiler secret and control handling is fail closed, but malformed-Unicode rejection is not preserved at the external prelaunch handoff boundary.
Canonical JSON and length-framed domain separation bind normalized goal, authority, candidates, blueprints, compilation, manifest, and package root.
No path was found for changed package content to retain the approved package digest; verification rerenders and requires canonical equality plus both external expectations.
Dynamic fences contain rendered JSON and schema-safe identifiers constrain headings and paths; no Markdown breakout was found before the stop.
The host boundary and exact launch bindings are explicit, but safeHandoffText accepts escaped lone surrogates and therefore does not fail closed under R20.
